title: Agnes water
summary: Beautiful right hand wave, punchy and barreling in the offshore. Not too crowded. 5/5 for this wonderful spot.

title: Manly
body: Manly is the most populated place in the nothern beaches. A lot of beginners in the water here, but also a lot of pros! When the condition are met, manly is home to some of the most powerful and giant waves around here. (Deadman's).
North steyn, the northern part of the beach, is where I had most of my sessions. While it's crowded, I got a nice few ones.

title: Freshie. Home spot
body: While it's a longboards wave mostly, freshie being my home spot (running from home with my board under the arm, I'd be in the water in 2 minutes) I still got tons of good boogy sessions here. Especially around Christmas when a bit swell hit us. There were waves up to 2/3 meters, it was the first real big waves I surfed in Australia.

title: Curl curl
body: When nowhere the waves are good, curly somehow still works!? But be aware, this is extremely crowded, and some localisms...

title: Mona Vale
body: Mona vale is a good spot but lot of current.

title: Bungan beach
body: Almost everywhere you can access by car, but bungan is one of the only places where you can't, which means it's filtering some of the lazy guys, and gives you a good chance to grabs some waves when you're hungry!

title: Newport, my home spot.
body: No matter how early you wake up, there's always someone in the water before you at Newport.
I could literally run to the spot from my bedroom at sunrise, it's always crowded!!
Given this was my home spot for a while, I had amazing sessions. The reef on the south offers a beautiful right that cam barrel with the good conditions.

title: Bilgola
body: A few sessions here, when it's too crowded everywhere else, you can get some waves here at Bilgola.

title: Avalon beach
body:
The famous Avalon beach. Home of Ben player! 
There are these breaks here, I mostly tried the north wave, but had a sesh south as well. There's also "little Avalon" that breaks further in, haven't tried. Good spot, gets crowded.

title: Whaly
body:
I had countless sessions at Whaly. 
The weekends get hectic, but it's always great fun. It's vanlife paradise territory, so it's always good to camp out and surf there.

title: Barrels @ Seal rocks
body: First sesh, very clean waves! Second early morning, lots of dolphins around! Got a few good waves but suddenly it stopped for over 30min, the water was strangely extremely cold!?

title: Scoots Head
body: Very long right, breaking way behind the rocks. I jumped in after a long walk on the reef, and found a big right, very long! The locals were very friendly and welcoming, what a great experience.

title: Duranbah
summary: Duranbah, a full blown secret spot
body: Turns out the left of d-bah is almost boogy only. Met a few guys in there, pretty nice left and good vibes.

title: Small lefts @ Mystics
body: Mystics is a famous boogy wave, a big left that is praised by riders in Australia. When I visited, there was only a small wave, but it was still fun.

title: Coolum
body: Early morning surf sesh, my instinct tells me to jump off the rocks into a break, and I was served with beautiful clean waves. Not too crowded, got a few good ones

title: Snapper rock
body:
Scary place! You need to jump in the water in a channel with rock on either side, and in a few seconds you're facing a 3m monstre, with lots of people on it.
Still, I had a lot of fun :)

title: Brunswick Head
summary: Jump in the river for a wild run
body: Definitely one of the highlights session of Australia. Brunswick had a real boogy community, I had several surf there and always met a few friends in the water. That day we had a big swell, and I had lots of fun in very big waves.
